description: Docs-layer section applicability and additions for review. Intended to migrate into dist/scope-library.json later.

default_bullets.text: Obtain monthly details of the Company's consolidated working capital and analyze and comment on:

children.text: The composition of individual working capital accounts (e.g., trade receivables, accounts payable and accruals, etc.);
Monthly working capital trends, metrics, and seasonality;
Large, unusual, or non-operating items that may have affected normal working capital trends (such as accruals for bonuses, capital accruals, month end vs quarter end differences, etc.); and
Understanding the accounting impact of allowance for uncollectible amounts and other accruals requiring judgment.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of existing and future capital cost requirements including:

children.text: Maintenance versus growth capital expenditures;
Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures.

default_bullets.text: Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off-balance sheet) presented by Management and other potential debt-like items;
Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt.
If required, obtain and read the auditor's working papers for the latest fiscal year.
Comment on the nature and volume of audit differences (recorded and unrecorded adjustments), use of accounting estimates, and any changes in accounting policies or methods.
Comment on control issues identified by the external auditors, if applicable.
Comment on any additional red flags or key risks identified in the provided audit materials.
Review audit committee reporting and presentations from the audit firm.
Obtain a comprehensive view of revenue and margins. Obtain underlying transactional detail, if available, and gain an understanding of major trends in revenue and margins, including:

children.text: Revenue and margin by significant customer, channel, and/or market segment, as applicable; and
Price, volume, and mix trends.

default_bullets.text: Discuss with management key drivers of revenue and margin over the Historical Period, including:

children.text: Major customer/contract wins or losses and timing impacts;
Non-recurring items affecting period results; and
Operational, market, or seasonal factors affecting demand and fulfillment.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and review an analysis of revenue quality and related balances, and inquire about:

children.text: Revenue recognition policies and any recent changes;
Billing and collection terms, including significant deviations from standard terms;
Credits, rebates, returns, and other contra-revenue items;
Concentration/dependency risk; and
Other unusual items affecting reported revenue or gross margin.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.rationale: Banking balance sheet analysis uses a sector-specific structure instead of generic AP/accrual procedures.

accounts_receivable.rationale: Banking balance sheet analysis uses a sector-specific structure instead of generic AR procedures.

operating_expenses.rationale: Banking operating expense review uses sector-specific expense categories and metrics.

revenue_analysis.rationale: Some industries use sector-specific income analysis instead of generic revenue/price-volume framing.

working_capital.rationale: Banking uses a sector-specific balance sheet profile rather than generic AR/AP-based working capital analysis.

accounts_receivable.text: Understand billing practices, including progress billings, retention, and contract assets/liabilities.
Review aging, collectability, disputes/claims, and change-order collectability.
Assess concentration and credit risk by customer and project.
Obtain and review work-in-progress (WIP) reporting and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Percentage-of-completion methodology and inputs (cost-to-complete, estimated gross margin);
Under/over billings and their drivers;
Job-level margin fade/gain and change-order tracking; and
Backlog composition, burn-off, and conversion assumptions.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Understand subcontractor payables, accrued costs, and cut-off practices.
Assess payroll/union accruals (if applicable), accrued project costs, and cut-off controls for subcontractor and supplier expenses.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Bonding capacity and surety requirements (if applicable).
Warranty obligations, claims, liquidated damages, and performance obligations, including project-specific disputes.
Long-lead materials exposure, escalation clauses, and related purchase commitments.

revenue_analysis.text: To the extent available, obtain and review an analysis of Target's revenues and margins and inquire about:

children.text: Revenue stream (civil work, special projects, services, and equipment rental);
Customer;
Geography; and
End market/industry.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze revenue, margin, pricing, and average project length on a project basis, where available.
Understand Target's job costing methodology, including margin components and periodic true-ups.

Understand Target's revenue recognition and billing process (for example: percentage-of-completion, periodic and contract-completion true-ups and variability, loss-provision process, policy controls/escalations, and lifecycle timing impacts on EBITDA).
Understand the profile and exposure of major projects/contracts, including customer concentration and key contractual risk terms.
Obtain and analyze backlog and active backlog as of the latest available date (including backlog margin on a project basis and historical backlog conversion).

working_capital.text: Obtain and review an analysis of working capital and inquire about the following on a combined basis:
Composition of individual working capital accounts to determine sufficient working capital needs of the Company (including bonding requirements).
Monthly trending and fluctuations in working capital and impact on reported metrics (for example: days sales/payables/inventory outstanding and net working capital as a percentage of revenues).

Large, unusual, or non-operating items and current/upcoming initiatives that may affect normalized working capital trends (such as exited businesses, settlements, capital projects, and non-trade balances).
Nature of the Company's unearned revenue.

accounts_receivable.text: Obtain and review an analysis of construction receivables and contract balances and inquire about:

children.text: Billed and unbilled receivables (including holdbacks/retentions) aging by project and major customer;
Under/over billings and contract asset/liability balances;
Disputes/claims and change-order collectability; and
Allowance for uncollectible accounts, write-offs, and reserve methodology.

accounts_receivable.text: Assess receivable concentration and credit risk by project, customer, and contract type.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Obtain and review an analysis of Target's accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:

children.text: Terms of trade with major suppliers, significant vendor allowances, rebates, and/or purchase commitments;
Trade payables aging analysis and days payable outstanding analysis;
Billings in excess of cost profile; and
Other current and non-current accrued liabilities.

prepaid_materials_and_other_assets.text: Prepare a summary of other assets (including prepaid materials and prepaid assets) and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations, and significant balances.

inventory.text: Prepare a summary of inventory and obtain an understanding of the Company's inventory costing methodology, inventory tracking, work-in-progress accounting interface, and obsolescence reserves.

quality_of_earnings.text: Provide an eyecare-specific quality of earnings analysis focused on incremental EBITDA risk areas, including:

children.text: Cash-to-accrual adjustments in billing and collections;
Physician compensation, procedure mix, and reimbursement/pricing effects; and
Pro forma impacts from de novo expansion or prospective add-on acquisitions, where applicable.

operating_expenses.text: Physician/provider compensation structure, staffing mix, and productivity-linked cost drivers.
Rent.
Professional fees.
Key supplier cost arrangements (for example: volume rebates and minimum commitments for major suppliers).
Customer acquisition expense trends (for example: marketing, promotion, and advertising).

working_capital.text: Perform eyecare-focused working capital analysis, including:

children.text: Monthly working capital trends, including seasonality (typically over the last 24 months, where available);
Historical trends in key working capital metrics (DSO, DIO, DPO), including payor-driven effects; and
Factors that may have distorted the current or historical working capital position.

accounts_receivable.text: Aging profile by payor category and major counterparty concentration.
Billing-to-cash conversion, denials, adjustments/write-offs, and collection trends.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's payables and accrued liabilities and inquire about:

children.text: Terms of trade with major suppliers and payables aging analysis;
Accrued liabilities specific to the practice model (including payroll/bonus, rent, and professional fees, as applicable).

net_debt.text: Analysis of remaining liabilities surrounding contingent consideration.

quality_of_revenue_and_receivables_and_c.text: Using the underlying billing data, develop a revenue waterfall analysis and understand the following:

children.text: Historical collection profile;
Variances between implied accrual revenue and the Company's internal records; and
Accounts receivable profile.

quality_of_revenue_and_receivables_and_c.text: Compare the Company's cash receipts and disbursements to bank statement inflows and outflows.

other_current_assets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's other current assets and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations, and balances potentially excluded from working capital (for example: income taxes recoverable and deferred finance costs).

accounting_overview.text: Discuss healthcare-specific accounting profile considerations with management, including:

children.text: Reporting methodology and management reporting structure;
Basis for cost allocations and shared services, if any;
Revenue recognition policies by service type and payor class, and any recent policy changes; and
Significant accounting estimates and non-recurring transactions.

quality_of_earnings.text: Summarize potential adjustments identified regarding the profit and loss performance of Target in the form of a quality of earnings analysis, summarizing the risks that may impact EBITDA, including:

children.text: Significant out of period revenue and expense (e.g., cash to accrual accounting impact); and
Pro forma impacts for items such as recent de novo.

revenue_analysis.text: Obtain and review center-level financial information, including key operating metrics, revenue, and EBITDA, and inquire about unusual trends or fluctuations over the historical period, including unusual or non-standard contracts.
Bridge revenue and profitability over the historical period by considering:

children.text: Same-location and de novo center performance;
Center-based versus home-based mix;
Procedure mix (including CPT-code-level analysis, where available);
Top patient and/or top payor concentration;
Changes in direct and indirect operating expenses;
Center-level expense trends;
Corporate overhead allocations, if any;
De novo start-up costs; and
Components of other income and expense that may impact EBITDA.

operating_expenses.text: Behavior therapists (registered and unregistered) and board-certified behavior analysts: compensation, benefits, incentive compensation, and turnover.
Non-provider salaries, wages, and benefits, including incentive compensation.
Owner compensation and benefits.
Treatment supplies, including key suppliers and purchasing terms.
Center-level costs and sub-leasing arrangements.
Insurance expense.
Selling, general, and administrative/corporate expenses.
Overhead allocations.

working_capital.text: Obtain and read an analysis of working capital on a monthly basis and inquire about historical trends, including seasonality, fluctuations in days revenue outstanding and days payable outstanding, working capital as a percent of trailing revenue, and other significant items.

accounts_receivable.text: Aging analysis by center and by payor.
Allowance for doubtful accounts and write-offs.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:

children.text: Accounts payable aging, terms of trade with major vendors, and days payable outstanding;
Accrued liabilities;
Deferred revenue; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Fixed-asset continuity schedules (including significant additions and disposals).

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Pending or threatened litigation or investigations by regulatory or other authorities; and

net_debt.text: Obtain, read, and discuss with management net debt and debt-like items, whether recorded on-balance sheet or off-balance sheet (for example: notes payable, capital vendor payables, deferred purchase price of property or services, accrued bonuses, and capital lease obligations), including expected future payout timing and amounts.

waterfall_revenue_analysis.text: If available, obtain patient/encounter-level detail from billing and collections systems to perform a claims-level revenue analysis reflecting net cash received on closed claims and estimated cash expected on open claims (based on historical reimbursement trends). Discuss with management:

children.text: Drivers of monthly/quarterly/annual net revenue trends by location, significant payor, and significant service code;
Reimbursement trends by payor, service, and location;
Pricing/volume trends by location, payor, and service;
Payor mix trends in total and by location;
Trends and drivers of fluctuation in the cash collection cycle;
Reconciliation of cash collections reported in the billing system to third-party bank statements (on a sample basis); and
Reconciliation of billing and collection information to financial statements.

waterfall_revenue_analysis.text: Bridge revenue for changes in patient volumes, same-store/de novo/acquired facilities, and changes in direct and indirect operating expenses.

revenue_analysis.text: Obtain and read an analysis of commission and fee income trends and recognition, including contingent commissions by carrier.
Assess service and other fee income, including cancellation provisions where applicable.
Evaluate treatment of interest income, commission splits, and revenue cut-off.

quality_of_earnings.text: Identify potential adjustments to reported earnings before interest, income taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) by considering the impact of:

children.text: The impact of acquisitions (for example: acquired businesses);
The impact of supply chain disruptions, if any;
The impact of research and development expenditures and capitalization policies;
The timing of true-ups and other adjustments made less frequently than monthly;
The impact of commodity/raw material cost fluctuations and any associated hedging programs;
The impact of key supplier cost arrangements, including assessment of hedged versus market prices and illustrative impacts to EBITDA; and
The impact of lease renewal.

operating_expenses.text: Compensation and benefits analysis by function (including headcount, incentive compensation, and key labor-cost drivers).
Occupancy, logistics, and manufacturing support cost trends.
Research and development and product quality/safety costs, including capitalization impacts where applicable.
Selling, general, and administrative cost profile, including fixed-versus-variable cost behavior.

working_capital.text: Payroll-related funding and cash timing impacts relevant to working capital normalization and peg setting.

inventory.text: Inventory composition and turnover by major product/category group.
Aging profile, slow-moving/excess/obsolete exposure, and reserve methodology.
Vendor allowances/rebates and purchasing practices that affect inventory valuation and margins.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Near-term required capex and deferred capex backlog, where applicable.

revenue_analysis.text: Revenue and gross-margin trends by plant, product category/SKU, customer, and channel, including concentration and mix effects.
Price-volume-mix and input-cost pass-through dynamics, including major raw-material and freight impacts on margins.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Accrued liabilities profile (including payroll, bonuses/incentives, and other significant operating accruals).

prepaids.text: Composition and trend analysis of significant prepaid balances.
Timing of cash outflows versus expense recognition for material prepaid categories.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Earn-out obligations and other contingent consideration from historical acquisitions, where applicable.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze revenue and gross margin trends by channel and major product category.
Assess retail-specific margin drivers, including promotional cadence, markdown architecture, returns behavior, and discounting mix.
Assess customer metrics where available (traffic, conversion, basket size, repeat rate/loyalty).

working_capital.text: Analyze inventory-related working capital drivers, including:

children.text: Inventory composition by category and location/channel;
Inventory turns, aged inventory, stock availability, and shrink/obsolescence trends;
Inventory valuation methodology and reserve adequacy; and
Returns, damages, and vendor return programs.

working_capital.text: Review vendor rebates/allowances and payment-term structures, and assess their impact on cash conversion.
Assess customer-liability working capital drivers, including gift card and loyalty program liabilities.

quality_of_earnings.text: Assess service-model earnings impacts not fully captured in common quality-of-earnings procedures, including:

children.text: Significant new/lost customer relationships and service-offering changes; and
Capacity and utilization effects from headcount vacancies or ramp-up/ramp-down.

revenue_analysis.text: Assess recurring-revenue quality metrics, including:

children.text: Annual recurring revenue composition;
Retention and churn trends; and
Customer relationship tenure patterns.

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of service-model operating expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Key components and trends of cost of revenue specific to service delivery (for example: software, data center, merchant service, and platform fees);
Capitalization of employee expenses, software/development costs, and commissions; and
Research and development cost profile and accounting treatment.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Assess deferred revenue / unearned revenue balances and recognition unwind profile.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Assess capitalized labour/software/commission trends and related amortization profile.

prepaid_expenses_and_other_assets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of the Company's prepaid and other assets and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations, and balances potentially excluded from working capital.

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of supermarket operating expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Store labour and support-function cost trends;
Information-systems and corporate overhead cost allocations, where applicable; and
Occupancy costs (including rent and lease-related cost drivers).

working_capital.text: Assess supermarket-specific working capital drivers by retail, wholesale, and corporate streams (to the extent separable), including:

children.text: Segment-level working capital composition and trade-ratio trends;
Inventory reserves, sales returns/allowances, and other judgmental accruals; and
Seasonality implications for cash flow and working capital peg setting.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Assess supermarket capex profile and requirements, including:

children.text: Growth versus maintenance capex for stores, renovations, eCommerce, IT/cybersecurity, distribution centers, and production units;
Committed capex and near-term spending versus budget; and
Historical and planned capital expenditure cadence.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of the technology business model and operating footprint, including:

children.text: Product and service portfolio with delivery/revenue model (for example: subscription, managed services, and project-based offerings);
Customer concentration and key end-market exposure; and
Dependencies on Parent/shared services, intercompany arrangements, and stand-alone separation points, where applicable.

accounting_overview.text: Understand incremental technology accounting considerations, including:

children.text: Recurring revenue, deferred revenue, and contract modification practices;
Capitalization/amortization of development costs and sales commissions; and
Stand-alone versus allocated/shared Parent cost treatment, where applicable.

quality_of_earnings.text: Assess technology-specific earnings quality considerations not fully captured in common quality-of-earnings procedures, including:

children.text: EBITDA-to-cash conversion in recurring-revenue models;
ARR/churn and booked-not-billed dynamics affecting run-rate earnings; and
Impact of product/development investment and capitalized labour on comparability.

revenue_analysis.text: Assess recurring-revenue quality and sustainability, including:

children.text: Reconciliation of billing/cash/GL and deferred revenue balances;
ARR and key recurring KPIs (new/lost/expansion customers, churn, and retention); and
Contract terms affecting price realization and revenue timing.

operating_expenses.text: Assess technology cost-base drivers, including:

children.text: Cloud/hosting/data-center and third-party platform/software cost trends;
Sales and customer-acquisition costs, including commission capitalization and amortization; and
Research and development / engineering spend mix between expensed and capitalized costs.

working_capital.text: Assess recurring-revenue working capital effects, including:

children.text: Deferred revenue and billing cadence implications for cash conversion;
DSO/DPO and receivable/payable aging trends, where relevant; and
Completeness of accruals and judgmental balances with monthly versus annual treatment.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of the telecom product/service portfolio, customer segments, and go-to-market channels.
Understand network and infrastructure model (owned versus leased) and key third-party dependencies.
Understand key commercial arrangements (for example: carriers, wholesale, and roaming), where applicable.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze telecom revenue quality and trends, including:

children.text: Revenue by service line and recurring versus usage-based components;
Churn, ARPU, net adds, retention, and related subscriber/customer KPIs; and
Pricing, discounting, promotions, and contract terms driving revenue sustainability.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Assess telecom capex profile and requirements, including:

children.text: Capex intensity and planned network/infrastructure investment programs;
Timing and drivers of capex deployment and deferral/backlog dynamics; and
Lease-versus-buy considerations for infrastructure and related cash-flow effects.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Assess telecom-specific commitments and contingencies, including:

children.text: Long-term network/site/capacity commitments and key vendor agreements;
Regulatory and licensing exposure (including spectrum-related commitments, where applicable); and
Material customer credits, service-level penalties, and dispute exposure.

revenue_analysis.text: Obtain and read an analysis of transportation revenue and margins and inquire about:

children.text: Revenue and profitability by customer, customer type, and route/trip type;
Volume and utilization KPIs (for example: trip counts/load factors), including seasonality effects; and
Transportation-specific cut-off timing considerations and non-recurring revenue components.

operating_expenses.text: Assess transportation operating-cost drivers, including:

children.text: Driver/labour costs and benefits;
Fuel, maintenance, repair, and other fleet operating costs; and
Fixed versus variable cost behavior across key expense categories.

working_capital.text: Assess transportation-specific working capital considerations, including:

children.text: Seasonality effects and cash-management procedures;
Trade-ratio trends and timing effects around receivables/payables; and
Normalization of significant non-recurring working capital items.
Fleet replacement cycle and related capital intensity;
Unit utilization and operating impact on replacement timing; and
Deferred/planned capex and impairment considerations.

net_debt.text: Summarize transportation-specific debt and debt-like items, including letters of credit, capital vendor payables, and fleet-related financing obligations.

inventory.text: Obtain and read an analysis of transportation inventory balances and inquire about:

children.text: Composition (for example: fuel, parts, supplies, and finished goods where applicable);
Costing methodology and turnover/aging; and
Provisioning for obsolete or slow-moving inventory.

accounting_overview.text: Discuss real-estate-specific accounting considerations with management, including:

children.text: Lease-related accounting policies (for example: straight-line rent, incentives/abatements, and common-area maintenance recoveries);
Tenant receivable provisioning and write-off policies specific to rent/recoveries; and
Investment property and joint-venture accounting/valuation approach, where applicable.

quality_of_earnings.text: Summarize potential adjustments to historical net operating income (NOI) on a consolidated basis by considering:

children.text: Management's proposed adjustments to reported NOI and supporting rationale;
Elimination of public company costs, where applicable; and
Potential impact of new, lost, acquired, developed, or sold properties and expiring leases.

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of the Company's operating expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Primary components of property operating costs, including property tax expense;
Rent bad debt expense trends; and
Other property-level cost drivers unique to the ownership/management model, where applicable.

balance_sheet.text: Summarize and understand Target's real-estate-specific balance sheet profile, including:

children.text: Rent receivable profile (including aging/provisions) and other non-trade receivable balances;
Tenant rental deposits and related liabilities;
Nature of investment properties and joint ventures; and
Any unusual real-estate-specific balance sheet items identified during due diligence.

business_overview.text: Read available information and meet with Target's officers in order to develop an understanding of Target, including its:

children.text: Organization structure and management reporting relationships;
Significant accounting estimates, including percentage-of-completion (POC) revenue;
Basis of financial information, including financial reporting, cost allocation methodologies, and consistency in accounting policies; and
Finance function.

quality_of_earnings.text: Subject to information availability, propose potential adjustments to historical earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) by considering:

children.text: Sustainability of margins (including the impact of changes in POC estimates on the Target's earnings);
Changes in Members'/Partners' remuneration;
Operational cash flows not captured within EBITDA (for example: capitalized software costs and capitalized leases);
Contract-level profitability estimate revisions and true-ups (for example: changes in estimates to complete under POC methodology).

revenue_analysis.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's revenue, direct expenses, and margins, and inquire about:

children.text: Gross-to-net revenue bridge, including payments to consultants and subcontractors;
Revenue and margins by key customer and project;
POC and typical gross-profit recognition patterns (for example: allocation of profits between project phases);
Key performance indicators (for example: proposal win rates, personnel availability/utilization rates, and billing rates);
Backlog, including timing of realization; and
Pipeline/opportunities, including win probabilities.

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's operating expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Headcount and personnel costs, including salaries, benefits, commissions, and incentive plans; and
Lease costs, including lease contracts close to expiry and related renewal/relocation plans.

working_capital.text: Comment on working capital trends/seasonality for the most recent trailing-twelve-month period and prior two to three fiscal years (based on information availability).
Identify sensitivities for cash flow management and normalize for items that may impact normal working capital trends.
Consider the impact of potential non-recurring items.

work_in_progress.text: Obtain a summary of work in progress (amounts recoverable on contracts) by project/customer and comment on unusual items, fluctuations, and significant balances.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Obtain and read an analysis of accounts payable and accrued liabilities, inquiring about accounts payable aging/days outstanding and accrued-liability profiles. Analyze the expected unwind of deferred income.

other_current_assets_liabilities.text: Understand the nature of other current assets and liabilities, and the valuation principles applied thereto.

net_debt.text: Summarize and comment on reported net debt items and other potential debt-like items within the transaction perimeter, including:

children.text: Lease liabilities;
Corporate taxes payable;
Pension liabilities;
Derivative financial instruments, if applicable;
Non-operating accruals;
Pending or threatened litigation or investigations; and
Commitments, contingencies, and other material off-balance-sheet transactions.

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's property, plant, and equipment capital expenditures and inquire about historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures, including:

children.text: Maintenance versus growth capex;
Other historical, deferred, and planned capex; and
Impairment write-downs and other potential issues.

accounting_overview.text: Understand incremental banking-specific accounting policies and practices, including:

children.text: Fair value measurement policies for financial instruments and related valuation considerations;
Expected credit loss provisioning methodology and key judgments;
Revenue recognition policies for net interest margin (including effective interest treatment);
Deferred placement/origination fees and capitalized commissions (including amortization approach);
Intercompany funding/transfer pricing allocation practices (if applicable); and
Accounting treatment for securitization assets/liabilities and related derecognition considerations (if applicable).

accounting_overview.text: Reconciliation of management internal reporting packages to audited financial statements, where available.
Revenue recognition for service and project contracts (including period-end true-ups) and related billing practices.
Intercompany transactions and cost allocation practices specific to branch/network operations.

quality_of_earnings.text: Assess HVAC-specific EBITDA normalization considerations, including:

children.text: Pricing pass-through timing and gross-margin lag effects from labour and commodity cost changes;
Cash-to-accrual timing differences and period-end true-ups affecting reported results;
Branch openings, closures, or acquisitions and related run-rate impacts;
Warranty, claim, and reserve movements that may affect normalized earnings; and
Other non-recurring operational events identified during diligence.

revenue_analysis.text: Revenue and margin trends by service offering (for example: service, installation/project, and maintenance contract streams).
Revenue and margin trends by customer end-market and geography.
Backlog and committed pipeline analysis, including conversion timing and cancellation risk.
Contract renewal/retention profile and effectiveness of cross-sell activity, where measurable.

operating_expenses.text: Direct cost drivers for field operations (for example: labour mix, subcontractor usage, materials, vehicles, and tools/equipment).
Branch-level occupancy, utilities, and other location operating costs.
Compensation structure and productivity metrics by role, including incentive compensation design.
Insurance and warranty-related cost trends and reserve adequacy considerations.

accounts_receivable.text: Aging and concentration analysis by major customer, including contract-related billing terms.
Reserve roll-forward, write-off trends, credit balances, and other collection adjustments.

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's operating expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Employee costs: headcount and staff compensation (salary, payroll tax, bonus, pensions, other benefits, key contractual terms), applicable personnel metrics, and any capitalization of such costs;
Stock options: impact of stock options and other non-cash incentive tools;
Commissions, finders' fees, securitization transaction costs, and related items;
Infrastructure and other technology direct expenses;
Research and development expenses and related capitalization;
Marketing and business development expenses;
Shared services cost allocations and description of services rendered, including allocation of costs within internal line-of-business management reporting, if applicable;
General and administrative costs, including allocation of costs within internal line-of-business management reporting;
Fixed versus variable costs; and
Unusual and extraordinary items.

balance_sheet.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's historical balance sheet items and comment on:

children.text: Mortgages, including mortgages under securitization and mortgages held for sale;
Securities;
Restricted assets;
Derivative assets;
Securitization assets and liabilities;
Deposits by category;
Debt and debt-like items, including:
Attribution of debt between funding for operations versus corporate debt;
Off-balance sheet items;
Cash collateralization obligations; and
Repurchase agreement obligations.
Working capital profile including receivables, payables, and accruals; and
Commitments and contingencies.

revenue_analysis.text: Gain an understanding of income streams by type, including:

children.text: Net interest income and margin - trends therein / evolution thereof, including:
Interest rate profile (fixed vs variable; differences by term / duration);
Product features (variable/floating and fixed);
Changes in maturity profile (including extensions of amortization periods and impact on yields); and
Cost of funding.
Non-interest income and non-interest margin - trends therein, key drivers, and one-time / non-cash items.
Other income (including fee income and commissions) - trends therein and noted one-time / non-cash items.

revenue_analysis.text: Analyze rental revenue drivers using the rent roll and supporting detail, including:

children.text: Occupancy, lease maturity schedule, and renewal/termination trends;
Base rent versus recovery income (for example: CAM, taxes, insurance, and utilities) and reconciliation to billings, where available; and
Concessions, abatements, and free-rent impacts on reported revenue.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Inquire about property-specific contingent exposures and obligations, including:

children.text: Property tax reassessments, appeals, and related reserves; and
Environmental compliance and remediation obligations, where applicable.

quality_of_earnings.text: Assess supermarket-specific EBITDA quality considerations, including:

children.text: Vendor allowances, rebates, and promotional funding and the timing of recognition relative to underlying purchase volume;
Shrink, spoilage, and inventory write-down policies and historical trends; and
Fuel, pharmacy, and other ancillary margin volatility drivers, where applicable.

revenue_analysis.text: Bridge revenue and gross margin by major category and store/channel, considering:

children.text: Same-store sales drivers (traffic and basket size) versus new store openings/closures;
Price versus volume mix and promotional intensity; and
Private-label versus branded mix and related margin impacts.

quality_of_earnings.text: Review investment income and portfolio-related earnings drivers, where applicable, including:

children.text: Asset allocation, duration, and credit quality of invested assets;
Realized and unrealized gains and losses and the basis of measurement; and
Sensitivity of investment income to interest rate movements.

working_capital.text: Evaluate billing and collection dynamics specific to time-and-materials and fixed-fee engagements, including:

children.text: Unbilled receivables and cycle time from time entry to invoicing;
Retainers and client deposits recorded as deferred revenue or other liabilities; and
Disputed billings and write-off trends by service line.
